message:
Fix: correct desperate fallback ID format to be parseable

The IdGenerator's desperate fallback mechanism, triggered when over
1000 collision attempts occur, was producing IDs in the format
`{prefix}-{hash}-{nonce}` (e.g., "bd-abc123-1001"). This format
contains a double-dash pattern that causes parse ambiguity - the
parser can't distinguish between the prefix-hash boundary and the
hash-nonce boundary.

Changed the format to `{prefix}-{hash}{nonce}` (e.g., "bd-abc1231001")
which:
- Maintains a single dash as the prefix-hash separator
- Appends the nonce directly to the hash without additional dashes
- Produces IDs that parse correctly as prefix + extended hash

This edge case is extremely rare in practice - it only triggers when
the database contains >1000 issues with identical title+timestamp
hashes (which would require malicious input or a broken clock).
However, when it does trigger, the IDs must still be valid.

Added test to verify the fixed format parses correctly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
